cards.title: Metrics: Explained
cards.bodyHtml:
Metrics are tracked weekly and daily. Both will come with scorecards so you can always stay up to date with how you are performing throughout the week. This decision was made because the daily scorecard will show the components that make up the averaged categories, such as handle time.

Metric Goals & What They Mean

QA Score — 90%

Leads and seniors manually review your interactions and grade them against a scorecard. This measures how well we are performing in our conversations with tutors.

Avg Adherence — >90%

Also called productive adherence — tracks your status changes. Was this agent where they were supposed to be? Discrepancies are investigated for Assembled reporting issues or agent support needs.

Avg Acceptance — 95%

How many interactions you accepted vs. how many were sent to you. Tracks chats only.

Avg Handle Time — Varies

How long you were in an interaction. Emails: tracked from first response to solve. Chats: begins upon assignment. LLP & Tutor Chat: 15 min · TES Tickets: 10 min · LLP Tickets: 20 min.

Solved Tickets — 95 / 100

95% of 100 tickets. You can turn your ticket count into a percentage because of how easy this metric is to calculate.

Behavior & Productive Scores — Weekly

Reflective of your metrics for the week and based on the goals above.

cards.title: Ticket Handling:: Admin Hub
cards.bodyHtml:
Moving forward, we will claim one ticket at a time.

1 — Claim an Unclaimed Ticket

We should no longer be sending the first reply message in tickets. We claim them and work to solve or ask the appropriate questions in order to assist.

2 — Set Status, Move onto Other Unclaimed or Waiting on Support Tickets

Once you have set the ticket to the appropriate status, check your previous tickets to ensure none are waiting on support. If they are, prioritize these before claiming unclaimed tickets.

Note The goal is to ensure we are staying on top of all communications, new and old.

cards.title: Breaks / Meals & Timing
cards.bodyHtml:
Since we track Productive Adherence, it is important to move between breaks, meals, and queue changes in a deliberate way.

  • Put yourself into the corresponding status in Zendesk 5 minutes prior to the change — Go into Break, or Go into Meal.
  • If you are struggling to get out of a chat, reach out to your senior or lead so we can determine if transferring the chat to a different agent is the right call.

cards.title: Cross Team Escalations
cards.bodyHtml:
Under no circumstances should we ever be transferring tickets to another team.

If a tutor reaches out about an issue we do not handle:

  • Get full details from the tutor on what they are experiencing.
  • Create a new ticket on behalf of the tutor for the correct team - many of these will be through the bat signal workflow.
  • Provide the tutor with the ticket ID.
Exceptions Applicants and placement requests should still be handled by using #transferchat. The placement team phone number is 888-315-5816.

Returning tickets: if another team sends us a ticket that is out of scope, we can send it back to them. [pending path to send client team tickets back]

cards.title: Escalation Path
cards.bodyHtml:
Utilize the work channel and LoDs prior to messaging or tagging a senior or lead.

Order of Escalation

  • Use the #fotf_work channel first.
  • @ a LoD second.
  • @ a senior third.
  • @ a lead fourth.

cards.title: Staying Up to Date
cards.bodyHtml:
There are several channels you should always be checking during your shift. It is mandatory to stay up to date throughout the day — this helps reduce traffic in our channels.

  • #fotf_work
  • #fotf_dailies
  • #the-fellowship-of-the-fix
  • #queue-health-and-announcements

Be sure to review previous messages as well — especially if you are reporting something that might be a bug.
